topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the Department of Veterans Affairs and its facilities, as well as regulations affecting government-sponsored enterprises.

1: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical center in Spokane, Washington, as the 'Mann-Grandstaff Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center.'
3: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from declaring as excess to the needs of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), or otherwise disposing or reducing the acreage of, federal land and improvements at the St. Albans campus, New York.
6: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) community based outpatient clinic in Mansfield, Ohio, as the 'David F. Winder Department of Veterans Affairs Community Based Outpatient Clinic.'

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at adjusting tax deductions and credits for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: extend through 2019 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable wind resources
allow such credit as an offset against the alternative minimum tax
Document 2: allow a taxpayer who uses a residence to conduct a trade or business a standard tax deduction
equal to the lesser of $1,500 or the taxpayer's gross trade or business income
Document 3: allow a taxpayers to elect to exclude from gross income, without limitation, earned income
income for services performed from sources within a foreign country
Document 4: exclude from gross income contributions to the capital of a partnership
set forth rules for determining the basis of property and money contributed to the capital of a partnership
Document 5: extend the period in which a corporation may carryover excess contributions
for purposes of the tax deduction for charitable contributions from 5 to 20 succeeding taxable years
Document 6: enable individual taxpayers to file their income tax returns directly through the Internal Revenue Service website
without the use of any intermediary
Document 7: make permanent the 15-year recovery period for qualified leasehold improvement property
for purposes of the tax deduction for depreciation

topic.name: Education Support Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and support for at-risk students and schools.

1: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to implement a five-year pilot program providing commodities to nonprofits for the provision of nutritious food to at-risk school children
providing commodities to nonprofits for the provision of nutritious food to at-risk school children on weekends and during extended school holidays
2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to encourage the establishment and operation of Teachers Institutes
established to improve the quality of teaching and learning through collaborative seminars
3: Amends the Safe and Drug-Free Schools and Communities Act to require states to use grants for safe and drug-free schools to collect and report information on the incidence of bullying and harassment
require local educational agencies and schools to use subgrants to prevent and respond to incidents of bullying and harassment
4: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to local educational agencies and nonprofit organizations to support the school reform efforts of public schools
Requires each grant to be used to address at least one of the following areas of school reform
5: Amends title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow local educational agencies to use title II subgrants to train school personnel in restorative justice and conflict resolution
6: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive matching grants to partnerships for the development of national model afterschool interdisciplinary education programs
Gives grant and subgrant priority to programs targeting a high percentage of impoverished, struggling, or rural students and their families

topic.name: Medicaid and Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the Medicaid program and various health care initiatives in the United States.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Provides supplemental funding for the qualifying individual (QI) program.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Provides supplemental funding for the qualifying individual (QI) program.
Document 3: Amends titles XVI (Supplemental Security Income) (SSI) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Require survivor annuity payments made to blind or disabled dependents to be disregarded in eligibility and benefit determinations under the SSI and Medicaid programs.
Document 4: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Require state Medicaid plan coverage of community-based attendant services and supports for certain Medicaid-eligible individuals.
Document 5: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Limit the penalty for late enrollment under part B to 10% and twice the period of no enrollment.
Document 6: Amends part A (General Provisions) of title XI of the Social Security Act
Authorize the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to waive applicable general and Medicaid requirements of the Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE).
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to extend through April 1, 2009, the approval of specified waivers under title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Approval of specified waivers under title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act for the MinnesotaCare Medicaid demonstration project.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States and related legislation to temporarily suspend duties on various goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain synthetic filament yarns.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous dispersions of poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) poly(styrenesulfonate).
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous catalytic preparations based on iron (III) toluenesulfonate.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on 3 Chloro 4 Methyl Aniline.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on certain sector mold press machines.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on reusable surgical drapes of textile materials.
Document 7: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to suspend the limitation on the period for which borrowers are eligible for loan guarantee assistance through December 31, 2013.

topic.name: Veteran Support Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at providing support, training, housing, and medical care for veterans.

Document 1: Authorize the city of Aurora, Colorado, to convey by donation to the United States nonfederal lands for construction of a veterans' medical facility.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Labor to pay to each covered veteran an assistance allowance for employment and training programs.
Document 3: Reduces the training program completion period for veterans' outreach program specialists.
Document 4: Prohibits the appropriation of funds by the VA for alternative energy projects without specific authorization.
Document 5: Directs the VA to transfer unobligated funds for veterans' medical care.
Document 6: Directs HUD to establish a pilot program to award grants for rehabilitating residences of disabled or low-income veterans.
Document 7: Directs DOD and VA to establish a uniform formulary for critical drugs for veterans.

topic.name: Firearm Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to the regulation, enforcement, and management of firearms in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2008 to repeal restrictions on the disclosure of the content of the Firearms Trace System database.
Requires the Attorney General to provide firearms information relating to law enforcement, national security, or intelligence purposes.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to provide that provisions imposing additional terms of imprisonment for using or carrying a firearm during and in relation to any crime of violence or drug trafficking crime shall not apply to a person if...
Defines 'peace officer' to mean an officer or employee of the federal government, or of a state or local government, who is authorized by law to carry a firearm.
Document 3: Impose a fine and/or prison term of up to 20 years for the sale, transfer, or barter of a stolen firearm or a firearm with an altered serial number.
Requires licensed firearms importers, manufacturers, or dealers to contact the national instant stolen gun check system before receiving firearms from any unlicensed person.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions and ensuring fair treatment in credit and mortgage services.

Document 1: Mortgage Assistance Information and Scam Prevention Act of 2009
notify the mortgagor of possible eligibility for federal homeowner assistance
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act
Prohibits a consumer credit card account from being terminated for inactivity
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
prohibit an insured depository institution from charging a fee to any consumer for receiving or using a debit card
Document 4: Universal Default Prohibition Act of 2009
prohibit a creditor from using any adverse information concerning any consumer
Document 5: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act
mandatory fee disclosures on automated teller machines (ATMs)
Document 6: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency'
Document 7: PLUS Loan Borrower Protection Act of 2008
specify an extenuating circumstance for purposes of determining if a borrower has an adverse credit history

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, conveyance, and protection of public lands and resources.

Document 1: Amends the Wild Free-Roaming Horses and Burros Act to direct the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture...
allow a state or an Indian tribe to assume all management and protection functions under the Act
Document 2: Authorizes the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to offer to enter into a binding agreement with the city of Tracy, California...
Requires the city to pay to the Administrator not less than the appraised fair market value of the parcel
Document 3: Permits Clark County, Nevada, to convey approximately 51 acres of land...
Provides for the reversion of such land to Clark County for management in accordance with the Southern Nevada Public Land Management Act of 1998
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the state of Alaska King Cove Road and Sitkinak Island...
Provides, upon conveyance of the non-federal land to the Secretary, for the designation of the wilderness additions to the Izembek and Alaska Peninsula Wildlife Refuges as wilderness.
Document 5: Grants the city of Mesquite, Nevada, the exclusive right to purchase certain parcels of public land...
Requires proceeds from certain sales to be used for the implementation of the Virgin River Multispecies Habitat Conservation Plan.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands and interests...
Requires the Connell Lake conveyance area, once conveyed, to be managed in accordance with the terms of the Recreation and Public Purposes Act.
Document 7: Authorizes the Utah National Parks Council of the Boy Scouts of America...
for the purpose of operating a camp in exchange for conveyance by Brian Head Resort of certain of its private land to the Boy Scouts.

topic.name: Political Campaign Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing transparency and regulating practices related to political campaigns and candidates.

Document 1: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions
prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls
Document 2: requires any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
shall be made publicly available at the same time and in the same manner as other reports and statements
Document 3: prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate from employing the spouse or any immediate family member of such candidate
Document 4: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate
Document 5: amends the Internal Revenue Code to authorize the FEC to disclose to the public the applicable tax returns of any person who has been nominated as a candidate of a major party
Document 6: prohibits the use by a recipient of Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) funds for lobbying expenditures or political contributions
requires the Secretary of the Treasury to develop and publish corporate governance principles and ethical guidelines

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various aspects of U.S. immigration policy, focusing on enforcement measures, eligibility for grants, and the treatment of specific groups within the immigration system.

Document 1: sanctuary city ineligible to receive...funds
Attorney General shall prioritize a request from the Secretary of Homeland Security
Document 2: amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act
verify the identity and employment eligibility of an employee
Document 3: law enforcement notification system
notify the identification bureaus of the state from which an alien is being released
Document 4: prohibits a sanctuary jurisdiction from receiving grants
amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase from two years to five years the maximum prison term
Document 5: adjust to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national
Adjustment applications must be filed not later than one year after the date of enactment
Document 6: permit a lawful alien who enters the United States on a nonimmigrant student visa
reimburses the local educational agency for the full, unsubsidized per capita cost
Document 7: permit multiple employers to submit a joint petition to import nonimmigrant H-2A visa temporary agricultural workers
An employer seeking to rehire H-2A workers may submit a simplified petition

topic.name: Military Personnel Security and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the security, mental health, and support systems for military personnel and their families.

Document 1: Enhance security of military personnel at DOD installations
submit to Congress a plan
Document 2: establish within each military department a board of review
ensure that covered individuals receive notification of their right to a separation review
Document 3: continue to provide pay and allowances to members of the Armed Forces
during a federal funding gap impacting the Armed Forces
Document 4: submit to Congress a policy for the development of a standard method for collecting, reporting, and assessing suicide data
involving members of the Armed Forces
Document 6: provide to the Secretary of Defense any intelligence information
indicates that a member of the Armed Forces has communicated with a person that seeks to harm the United States

topic.name: Regulatory Framework for Drug Approval
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at amending the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to improve the processes and regulations surrounding drug approval, compounding, and access to medications.

Document 1: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow drugs to be compounded (manufactured at a small scale) and distributed to practitioners for treatment of, or administration to, patients of that practitioner.
Compounded drugs must comply with United States Pharmacopeia standards.
Document 2: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to encourage the health care facilities of the Department of Defense, the Department of Veterans Affairs, and the Indian Health Service to report on antibacterial drug use.
The FDA must issue guidance on demonstrating the safety and effectiveness of such drugs.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to disqualify from being a 'first applicant' an applicant submitting an abbreviated new drug application (for a generic drug) to the Food and Drug Administration.
'First applicant' is expanded to include applicants that did not submit an application on the first day an application was submitted.
Document 4: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the manufacturer of a brand name drug from authorizing or commencing the manufacturing, marketing, selling, or distributing of a generic version of the drug.

from the time the manufacturer is notified by a generic manufacturer that a generic drug application has been submitted until the expiration or forfeiture of the 180-day exclusivity period granted to the generic manufacturer.
Document 5: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish a reciprocal marketing approval process that allows for the sale of a drug, biological product, or medical device that has not been approved by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) if the product is approved for sale in another country.
The FDA must grant or decline reciprocal marketing approval not later than 30 days after receiving a request.
Document 6: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require an application for the approval of a medical device or a product development protocol to include: (1) a description of any pediatric subpopulations that suffer from the disease or condition that the device is intended to treat, diagnose, or cure.
Requires the Secretary, acting through the Commissioner of Food and Drugs, to establish a publicly accessible database of all studies and surveillance of medical devices.
Document 7: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to permit the Food and Drug Administration to allow the sponsor of a new drug or biological product for the treatment of a rare, serious condition to rely upon information submitted for an approved medication that uses the same technology.
the new medication must use nucleic acids (e.g., DNA) or similar chemicals, or must affect a disease-causing product of a mutated gene.

topic.name: Youth Mental Health and Autism Research
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at improving mental health research, intervention services, and support for youth, particularly focusing on autism spectrum disorders and trauma-related issues.

Document 1: Youth Mental Health Research Act authorizes the establishment of a Youth Mental Health Research Network.
Requires the Director to ensure that an appropriate number of awards go to entities that focus on early detection and intervention of severe mental illness in young people.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to establish the National Institute of Autism Spectrum Disorders.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish grant programs related to autism spectrum disorders.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Education and HHS to award competitive matching grants to support services for early childhood educators.
Encourages cross-sector training opportunities for teachers and staff working in various early childhood programs.
Document 6: Requires HHS to support programs for the National Child Traumatic Stress Initiative.
Focuses on the development of services to prevent the long-term consequences of child trauma.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at supporting agricultural production, rural development, and food distribution in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to conduct a pilot program to purchase fresh fruits and vegetables for schools.
distribution to schools and service institutions participating in food service programs under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act.
Document 2: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities.
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity for a crop year by one acre for each acre used for the production of fruits or vegetables.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 to include shellfish as an eligible crop in the noninsured crop disaster assistance program.
includes shellfish producers in the emergency assistance for livestock program.
Document 4: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing.
has a serious lack of mortgage credit for lower and moderate-income families.
Document 5: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities.
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity for a crop year by one acre for each acre used for the production of fruits or vegetables.
Document 6: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat.
the effective price shall equal the sum of the higher of the national average market price received by producers during the 12-month marketing year.
Document 7: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing.
classified as 'rural' or a 'rural area' before October 1, 1990.

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Foreign Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and regulating foreign relations, particularly concerning Iran, Belarus, South Sudan, and Ukraine.

Document 1: report to Congress on cooperation between Iran and Russia
how cooperation between Iran and Russia affects U.S. national security
Document 2: prohibits the President from issuing a license for offshore U.S. dollar clearing for transactions involving Iran
Department of the Treasury shall report to Congress on financial institutions involved with Iran
Document 3: prohibits the Department of the Treasury from issuing a license for offshore dollar clearing involving Iran
Document 4: U.S.-imposed sanctions with respect to Belarus remain in effect
President shall certify to Congress conditions under the Belarus Democracy Act
Document 5: recognizes failure of leadership in South Sudan
sanctions imposed on individuals related to South Sudan shall remain in effect
Document 6: authorizes the President to provide Ukraine with defense articles and services
increased military interactions of the U.S. Armed Forces with Ukraine
Document 7: directs the President to conduct a nuclear policy review
requires Secretary of Defense to review U.S. nuclear posture

topic.name: Retirement and Disability Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to retirement, disability, and pension benefits for various groups, including widows, veterans, and public safety officers.

Document 1: increases in widow's and widower's insurance benefits
treatment of delayed retirement
Document 2: federal retirement benefits to U.S. citizens
service prior to 1977
Document 3: Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
board of directors composition and duties
Document 4: permit the plan sponsor of a multiemployer pension plan
amortize net investment losses
Document 5: reduce from 10 to five the number of years of marriage
divorced spouse to be eligible for benefits
Document 6: amortize net investment losses incurred from qualified fraudulent investment schemes
over a 40-year period
Document 7: payment of disability benefits
public safety officers who became permanently and totally disabled

topic.name: Transportation Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving various aspects of transportation systems in the United States, including aviation, rail, and infrastructure.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to establish an Aviation Rulemaking Committee to develop recommendations for the Administrator to include in a final rule to minimize flight attendant fatigue.
Requires the Committee to comprise aviation safety experts, labor representatives, and industry stakeholders.
Document 2: Directs the National Railroad Passenger Corporation (Amtrak) to propose a pet policy that allows passengers to transport domesticated cats or dogs on certain Amtrak trains.
Requires Amtrak to collect a fee for each cat or dog transported by a ticketed passenger to cover administrative costs.
Document 3: Directs the city of Los Angeles to transfer the LA/Ontario International Airport to the city of Ontario, California, within 60 days of a determination by the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) authorizing such transfer.

Declares that if the transfer does not occur within the 60-day period, the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) shall be prohibited from approving an airport improvement project (AIP) grant to the Los Angeles World Airports (LAX) until such transfer takes place.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to issue regulations directing each air carrier to establish a policy to ensure that a family that purchases tickets for a flight is seated together during that flight.
Defines 'family' to mean a grouping of individuals that, at a minimum, includes a child age 12 years or younger and an individual age 18 years or older.
Document 5: Amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Equity Act: A Legacy for Users (SAFETEA-LU) to make eligible for use of a state's apportionment of coordinated border infrastructure program funds certain improvements for the safe movement of freight and passenger rail at or across the borders between the United States and Canada and the United States and Mexico.
Reauthorizes the program through FY2015, indexed for inflation.
Document 6: Eliminates certain grounds for waiving Buy American requirements that the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) obligate federal funding for certain air navigation, highway, and rail activities and projects only if U.S. steel, iron, and manufactured goods are used in such projects.
Repeals the Secretary's authority to exempt Amtrak from Buy American requirements in certain circumstances.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to make capital grants to a class I, class II, or class III railroad or a state or local government for freight rail economic development projects.
Prescribes prevailing wage rate requirements for such projects in accordance with the Davis-Bacon Act.

topic.name: U.S. Fisheries Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation and management of various fishery resources in U.S. waters.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add New York State as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery and report to Congress.

Prohibits the Secretary from promulgating any interim rule under specified provisions of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act that prohibits fishing in the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery before such report demonstrates the need for interim or final rules.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery and report to Congress.

Prohibits the Secretary from promulgating any interim rule under specified provisions of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act that prohibits fishing in the South Atlantic red snapper fishery before such report demonstrates the need for interim or final rules.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the South Atlantic red snapper fishery and report to Congress.
Prohibits any interim rule that was promulgated before enactment of this Act from having any force or effect.
Document 5: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to require the Secretary of Commerce to annually make available to the New England Fishery Management Council, for the funding of Council research and monitoring priorities, all sums received by the United States as fines, penalties, and forfeitures of property for violations of any provision of this Act.
Document 6: Amends the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act to direct the Secretary of Commerce to urge international fishery management organizations to which the United States is a member to adopt shark conservation measures.

topic.name: Labor and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to labor rights, employment regulations, and anti-discrimination protections.

Document 1: collective bargaining rights for students enrolled at a private institution of higher education
perform work for remuneration at the institution's direction
Document 2: civil service employees to be hired on an at-will basis
employee may appeal to the Merit Systems Protection Board
Document 3: increase the frequency of collection of information on the sexual orientation and gender identity
National Violent Death Reporting System
Document 4: moratorium on federal agencies taking any regulatory rulemaking action
review of each rule that became effective after October 1, 1991
Document 5: expand the categories of prohibited discrimination when extending credit
discrimination on account of sexual orientation or gender identity
Document 6: invalidates the Dear Colleague Letter on Transgender Students
treatment of transgender students under the prohibitions against sex discrimination
Document 7: amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938
employment of an employee age 16 or 17 exclusively by a parent

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to telecommunications, internet access, and consumer protection in the digital age.

Document 1: Permanent ban on state and local taxation of Internet access.
Prohibition of multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce.
Document 2: Requires FCC to examine advanced blocking technologies.
Encourages parental empowerment tools for content filtering.
Document 3: Requires telecommunication carriers to provide call location information to law enforcement.
Focus on emergency situations for public safety.
Document 4: Reevaluation of spectrum plans by the FCC.
Involves reconfiguration for small geographic areas.
Document 5: Mandates NTIA to post transition plans for spectrum relocation.
Requires sharing and coordination of frequencies with commercial licensees.
Document 6: Extends the expiration of digital television coupons.
Applies to coupons issued after the enactment.
Document 7: Grants for training court reporters in realtime writing.
Promotes recruitment and placement in closed captioning.

topic.name: Information Disclosure and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring the proper disclosure, access, and security of sensitive information held by federal agencies and entities.

Document 1: submission of information by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
permitted disclosure under regulations
Document 2: requires the Government Publishing Office to establish a website
free electronic access to all congressionally mandated reports
Document 3: requires individuals, corporations, or other non-government entities to implement an information security program
notify consumers of certain data breaches
Document 4: require federal agencies to make disclosable public records available in an electronic format
presumption of openness
Document 5: establish an opt-out list of consumers
notify consumers publicly of such a breach
Document 6: unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure
liable to any person whose personally identifiable covered information has been disclosed
Document 7: provide individuals who have been affected by a breach of personnel records
establish a task force to develop specific recommendations on mitigating adverse consequences

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation and Military Activities
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation that addresses the intersection of wildlife conservation efforts and military readiness activities.

Document 1: establish Southern Sea Otter Military Readiness Areas for national defense purposes
monitor the Areas, at least annually, to evaluate otter status
Document 2: Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act
set the cost for Federal Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamps
Document 3: prohibit any regulation that goes in effect after February 24, 2014, from prohibiting or restricting the possession, sale, delivery, receipt, shipping, or transportation of elephant ivory
changing any methods of, or standards for, determining if ivory has been lawfully imported
Document 4: prohibits the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) or the Secretary of the Interior from implementing or limiting any modification to a public or private land-related policy
listing of the lesser prairie chicken as a threatened species
Document 5: requires the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce to provide to affected states all data that is used as the basis of a determination on whether a species is an endangered species
data submitted by a state, tribal, or county government
Document 6: extends financial and technical assistance to the states for the promotion of hunting and recreational shooting
recruit or retain hunters and recreational shooters
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of a military department to make payments to a conservation banking program
engaged or proposing to engage in military testing, operations, training, or another military readiness activity

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting and regulating small businesses in various aspects such as innovation, financing, and compliance.

1: Requires each federal agency to establish a plan for the periodic review of its rules that have a significant economic impact on small entities.
Sets forth criteria for review of a rule, including the continued need for the rule and the economic impact on small entities.
2: Requires the Chief Counsel for Advocacy of the Small Business Administration to submit an analysis on the investment of small businesses in developing new inventions.
Impact of patent ownership for small businesses on attracting outside investment and creating new jobs.
3: Reinstates the authority for Small Business Administration low-interest refinancing of small business debt.
Effective during any fiscal year in which the federal government's cost in making loan guarantees under such program is zero.
4: Reauthorizes and makes permanent the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs.
The national small business goal for federal agency research and development programs shall be at least 10%.
5: Requires the SBA to give priority to small business investment company applications for licenses to operate in a declared major disaster area.
The SBA shall not include the cost basis of any investment made by an SBIC in a small business concern in a major disaster area.
6: Requires the SBA to provide to the Defense Acquisition University a list of all changes made in the prior year to regulations affecting federal acquisition.
Materials developed to assist federal agencies or small businesses to comply with such regulations.
7: Directs the Secretary of Labor to establish a program of grants to eligible small business employers.
Grants to recent graduates to enable them to defray the cost of further education in relevant subjects.

topic.name: Designations of Public Facilities
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures that designate specific public facilities, including post offices and courthouses, as well as land exchanges involving public lands.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2300 Scenic Drive in Georgetown, Texas, as the 'Kile G. West Post Office Building.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 123 11th Avenue South in Nampa, Idaho, as the 'Herbert A Littleton Postal Station.'
Document 6: This bill designates as the Alex Diekmann Peak the 9,765-foot peak located 2.2 miles west-northwest of Finger Mountain on the western boundary of the Lee Metcalf Wilderness in Montana.
Document 7: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 501 East Court Street in Jackson, Mississippi, as the 'Charles Clark United States Courthouse.'

topic.name: Election Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving election processes, enhancing voter rights, and preventing foreign influence in American elections.

Document 1: Responsible Online Voter Empowerment Act of 2010
establishing an online voter registration program
Document 2: Keeping Foreign Money Out of Politics Act of 2015
prohibit foreign nationals from making contributions
Document 3: Restoring Confidence Through Smarter Campaigns Act
limit expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives
Document 4: amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002
prohibit a challenge to an individual's eligibility to register to vote
Document 5: Weekend Voting Act
establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as election days
Document 6: Voters' Right to Know Act of 2009
require reporting of phone bank costs and funding sources
Document 7: Prohibiting Foreign Influence in American Elections Act
apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nations

topic.name: U.S. Trade Policy and Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming U.S. trade practices, addressing currency valuation, and protecting intellectual property rights in international trade.

Document 1: Directs the United States Trade Representative (USTR) to pursue World Trade Organization (WTO) negotiations
Requires a USTR report to Congress on whether such goals have been met in revising the WTO rules
Document 2: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the administering authority to determine whether the exchange rate of the currency of an exporting country is fundamentally misaligned
Take certain actions under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding to offset such misalignment
Document 4: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to apply countervailing duties to nonmarket economies
Requires congressional approval for revocation of nonmarket economy country determinations made by the administering authority
Document 6: Directs the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) to report to Congress annually on the estimated annual loss of revenue to holders of U.S. intellectual property rights
Requires the USTR to impose duties on merchandise from China in an amount equivalent to the estimated annual loss of revenue

topic.name: Indigenous Rights and Governance
topic.description: This cluster discusses various acts and amendments related to the governance, rights, and welfare of Indigenous peoples in the United States.

Document 1: declares the landward boundary of the exclusive economic zone for marine fisheries management
vests the authority to manage fisheries in states and interstate marine fisheries management commissions
Document 2: reauthorizes appropriations for programs that promote the economic and social self-sufficiency of Native Americans
improves the capability of their governing bodies to regulate environmental quality
Document 3: establishes the U.S. Office of Hawaiian Relations
recognizes the right of qualified Native Hawaiian constituents to reorganize their governing entity
Document 4: prohibits the term 'marriage' from including relationships not recognized by the state
defines 'spouse' based on state recognition of marriage
Document 5: prohibits the term 'marriage' from including relationships not recognized by the state
defines 'spouse' based on state recognition of marriage
Document 6: extends housing assistance for Native Americans and loan guarantees for Native Hawaiian housing
makes the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands eligible for loan guarantees
Document 7: permits Native residents to organize as Urban Corporations
authorizes Urban Corporations to establish a settlement trust to promote health, education, and welfare

topic.name: Health and Social Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving health, safety, and social services for vulnerable populations, particularly children and minority communities.

Document 1: Communities of Color Teenage Pregnancy Prevention Act of 2011
grants for programs to provide youth in racial or ethnic minority or immigrant communities
Document 2: Breastfeeding Promotion Act of 2011
lactation as protected conduct under the Civil Rights Act
Document 3: Strengthening Community Safety Act of 2010
grants to first responder agencies for costs incurred due to reservists being on active duty
Document 4: Report the Costs Act
annual reports of federal agency chief financial officers to include the cost of multilingual services
Document 5: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
protections for credit reports of minor consumers
Document 6: Protect Children from Theft Act of 2015
require consumer reporting agency to create a blocked file for the minor
Document 7: Taxpayers' Freedom of Conscience Act of 2011
prohibits federal funds for population control or family planning activities

topic.name: Postal Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and policies related to the operations, funding, and services of the United States Postal Service (USPS).

Document 1: Synthetics Trafficking and Overdose Prevention Act of 2016 or the STOP Act of 2016
amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make the Postmaster General the importer of record for non-letter class mail
Document 2: Post Office Transparency Act - Makes procedures governing the closure or consolidation of postal branches the same as those applicable to post offices.
Document 3: Perpetual POW/MIA Stamp Act - Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp that honors the sacrifices of the members of the Armed Forces
Document 4: Postmark Payment Act of 2014 - Deems any payment required to be made on or before a prescribed date that is delivered by the USPS after such date to be received by the payee on the date of the U.S. postmark
Document 5: Semipostal Stamp Clarification Act of 2016 - removes the authority of the USPS to issue semipostal stamps except as provided by an Act of Congress
Document 6: Protect Overnight Delivery Act - Repeals the service standards for market-dominant products implemented by the USPS
Document 7: Supply Our Soldiers Act of 2007 - Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide postal benefits to a member of the Armed Forces

topic.name: Surface Mining and Environmental Protection
topic.description: Legislation related to the reclamation and management of abandoned mines and environmental protections in mining areas.

Document 1: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977 to extend a state's shield from federal liability for costs or damages incurred while implementing a state abandoned mine reclamation plan.
state's own reclamation activities conducted pursuant to an approved plan.
Document 3: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977, with respect to the allocation of surface mining reclamation funds to states and Indian tribes.
authorize uncertified states and Indian tribes to use certain unexpended and unappropriated balance amounts for acid mine drainage abatement and treatment.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Commissions
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and commissions established to honor individuals, reform programs, and manage federal operations.

Document 1: promote recreational opportunities for youth
commemorate women's history in the city of Tulare
Document 2: family of a deceased law enforcement officer
request a U.S. flag flown over the Capitol
Document 3: increase the number of positions under the Architect of the Capitol
specify multiyear contracts for property acquisition
Document 4: establishes an independent Program Reform Commission
report to Congress with recommendations for termination, modification, or retention
Document 5: establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission
plan and carry out activities to honor Ronald Reagan
Document 6: authorize U.S. Capitol Police officers to enforce immigration laws
make arrests on Capitol Grounds
Document 7: requires flags acquired for federal use to be manufactured in the U.S.
entirely grown, produced, or manufactured in the United States

topic.name: Pipeline Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory measures aimed at ensuring the safety and integrity of gas pipeline infrastructure.

Document 1: amends the Natural Gas Act
civil penalties for violations
Document 2: accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping
best available technology to detect gas leaks
Document 3: require the person purchasing or operating a gas or hazardous liquid pipeline facility to inspect it
ensure that such an inspection is conducted
Document 4: accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping
best available technology to detect gas leaks
Document 5: accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping
best available technology to detect gas leaks
Document 6: require the operator of a drilling and production operation to submit annually
civil penalty for non-compliance
Document 7: prohibit embezzling, stealing, obtaining by fraud
civil and criminal penalties for violations

topic.name: Support and Protection for Families
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing support and protection for families, including health coverage, financial assistance, and prevention of abuse.

Document 1: Prohibits terminating the eligibility of an individual who qualifies for treatment as a dependent under TRICARE solely by reason of exceeding the age limit of 21 years
Requires the continuation of such coverage for the period beginning on the date of enactment of this Act
Document 2: Repeal the denial to drug felons of eligibility for benefits under the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) program
Document 3: Requires a state that is receiving funds for certain law enforcement assistance programs to adopt laws and policies that prohibit parole for any individual who is convicted of a criminal sexual offense against a victim who is elderly
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish the local food for healthy families program to make grants to specified entities to provide low-income families with incentives to purchase fruits and vegetables
Document 5: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to increase milk income loss contract payments
Document 6: Redefine 'natural disaster declaration' for purposes of the livestock compensation program
Document 7: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision or other practice is adopted

topic.name: Regulatory Acts in Health and Safety
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts focused on health, safety, and regulatory standards in food and medical sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to identify and establish standards for contaminants in bottled water
Requires the Secretary to make certain additional information available to the public about the regulation of contaminants in bottled drinking water.
Document 2: Amends the Egg Products Inspection Act to revise housing and treatment of egg-laying hens and related enforcement provisions.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to delegate certain enforcement authority with respect to eggs produced in California.
Document 3: Sets forth additional factors for the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to consider in determining the payment amount for new clinical diagnostic laboratory tests
Requires the Secretary to justify publicly the basis and rate determination for any payments made under the clinical laboratory fee schedule for new clinical diagnostic laboratory tests.

topic.name: Immigration and Refugee Assistance
topic.description: Legislation focused on immigration policies, refugee assistance, and support for specific groups affected by historical events.

Document 1: Makes 3,000 immigrant visas available to individuals who were born in Tibet;
available to the sons, daughters, grandsons, or granddaughters of such individuals.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to provide for the granting of citizenship as of April 2, 2009;
was not culpable for an act that was a cause of an injury during such shooting.
Document 4: Makes 3,000 immigrant visas available to individuals who were born in Tibet;
available to the sons, daughters, grandsons, or granddaughters of such individuals.
Document 5: Establishes the Commission on Wartime Relocation and Internment of Latin Americans of Japanese descent;
recommend appropriate remedies to Congress based on preliminary findings.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health systems, surveillance, and safety standards in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prescribe minimum safety standards
Requires DOT inspection and testing of intrastate gas pipelines
Document 2: Establish the Epidemiology and Laboratory Capacity Grant Program
Issue guidelines for public health entities relating to information systems
Document 3: Establish the Epidemiology and Laboratory Capacity Grant Program
Address documented workforce shortages in state and local health departments
Document 4: Establish the Epidemiology and Laboratory Capacity Grant Program
Evaluate best practices in public health surveillance
Document 5: Establish an advisory council on prostate cancer
Carry out a national campaign to increase the awareness and knowledge of prostate cancer
Document 6: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to prescribe rules prohibiting unfair or deceptive acts
Require price information to be disclosed clearly and conspicuously
Document 7: Establish a program to make grants to accredited schools of allopathic or osteopathic medicine
Increase the number of recent allopathic and osteopathic medical school graduates who practice in underserved rural communities

topic.name: Climate Change and Disaster Preparedness
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving preparedness for climate-related disasters and promoting awareness and education on climate change impacts.

Document 1: Tsunami Warning and Education Act
monitoring and evaluating earthquakes
Document 3: Global Warming Education Program
understanding of human induced global warming
Document 4: geopolitical effects of global climate change
implications of such effects on U.S. national security
Document 5: earthquake response plan
improve the ability of trauma center hospitals
Document 7: climate change
control of air pollution

topic.name: Local Governance and Community Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at empowering local governance, enhancing community support systems, and improving urban planning and housing assistance.

Document 1: Revises requirements for the apportionment of state planning funds to metropolitan planning organizations (MPOs).
Defines 'consolidated metropolitan planning organization' to mean a sole MPO that serves a metropolitan statistical area.
Document 2: Amends the Housing and Urban Development Act of 1968 to reauthorize and revise requirements for assistance with respect to housing for low- and moderate-income families.
Requires specified mortgagee notices to homeowners in the event of mortgage payment delinquencies.
Document 3: Directs the Corporation for National and Community Service to award competitive, matching, three-year grants to nonpartisan, collaborative, public-private partnerships at the state and local level for efforts to grow and strengthen direct-service youth mentoring programs.
Requires partnership grantees to use at least 80% of their grant for competitive subgrants to mentoring programs.
Document 4: Requires each federal agency to publish in the Federal Register a plan for the periodic review of its major rules.
Requires the review to include a cost-benefit analysis of the rule.
Document 5: Establishes the Bipartisan Earmark Reform Commission to develop a clear definition of what constitutes an earmark.
Requires the Majority Leaders of the House of Representatives and of the Senate to introduce the legislation recommended in the report.
Document 6: Establishes the Senate Committee to Reduce Government Waste to research, review, and study federal programs that are underperforming or nonessential.
Recommend to the Senate actions designed to modify or eliminate them.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to make sure that advance notice of a covert test of a transportation security system is not provided to any individual before completion of the test.
Directs the Secretary to study the impact of implementing covert testing procedures on the Department's efforts to improve transportation security.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Risk Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to agricultural marketing, pricing, and risk management in the context of dairy and livestock industries.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act
direct the Secretary of Agriculture to base the minimum Class II milk price on the average production cost
Document 2: Amends the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946
establish mandatory reporting for wholesale pork cuts
Document 4: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to include coverage for losses to an insured commodity
when, as a result of a federally-imposed quarantine, the commodity must be destroyed
Document 3: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 to extend the Terrorism Risk Insurance program
Requires reports in 2017, 2020, and 2023 on the findings of the President's Working Group on Financial Markets

topic.name: Health Policy for Pacific Islanders
topic.description: This cluster focuses on health policy initiatives and legislation aimed at addressing the health care needs of Native Hawaiians and Other Pacific Islanders in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and implement a national strategy for identifying and evaluating the health status and health care needs of Native Hawaiians and Other Pacific Islanders.
Requires the Secretary to conduct a preliminary health survey to identify the major areas and regions in which NHOPI people reside.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and implement a national strategy for identifying and evaluating the health status and health care needs of Native Hawaiians and Other Pacific Islanders.
Requires the Secretary to conduct a preliminary health survey to identify the major areas and regions in which NHOPI people reside.

topic.name: Homeownership Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting and facilitating homeownership through various support programs and regulatory amendments.

Document 1: Homeownership Preservation Education Act of 2012
reduce defaults on residential mortgages
Document 2: Second Chance at Homeownership Act of 2012
create a voluntary FHA program to provide lenders for qualified second-chance borrowers
Document 5: Honest FHA Originator Act of 2009
mortgagee eligibility criteria
Document 6: Protecting Habitat Homeownership Act
exempt residential mortgage loans originated by non-profit low-income housing providers

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing accountability, oversight, and protection in government programs and services.

Document 1: North Texas Zebra Mussel Barrier Act of 2012 addresses environmental protection and accountability in water management.
Document 2: Military Separation Pay Fairness Act of 2014 ensures fair treatment and accountability for former military members.
Document 3: Court-Appointed Guardian Accountability and Senior Protection Act emphasizes oversight in guardianship proceedings.
Document 4: Bill prohibits actions on proposed child support enforcement rules, indicating oversight in social services.
Document 5: IRS program for identity protection for adopted children highlights accountability in tax benefits.
Document 6: Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability aims to assess effectiveness of federal agency programs.
Document 7: Regulations on Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac focus on oversight of housing finance and mortgage practices.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative efforts aimed at increasing government accountability, transparency, and efficiency in regulatory actions and agency operations.

Document 1: Defines a 'covered civil action' as a civil action seeking to compel agency action and alleging that an agency is unlawfully withholding or unreasonably delaying an agency action.
Requires an agency against which a covered civil action is brought to publish the notice of intent to sue and the complaint in a readily accessible manner.
Document 2: This bill requires the abolishment of the Departments of Agriculture, Commerce, Education, Energy, Health and Human Services, Housing and Urban Development, Labor, the Interior, the Treasury, Veterans Affairs, and Transportation on specified dates.
The head of each agency which is scheduled to be abolished must report to Congress on the roles and responsibilities of the agency, detailing the agency's justification for existence.
Document 4: Establishes the Department of Commerce and the Workforce for the advancement of economic growth and job opportunities.
Consolidates various agencies to streamline operations and improve efficiency.

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing legal procedures and protections for individuals involved in criminal justice and employment discrimination cases.

Document 1: Revises provisions concerning pre-trial investigations of charges and specifications under the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ)
Prohibits the purpose of the investigation to be to serve as a discovery tool for the accused.
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to make competitive grants to state, tribal, and local governments to establish or maintain programs that provide protection or assistance to witnesses
Evaluate grant applicants based on specified criteria, including the prevalence of witness intimidation.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to make competitive grants to state, tribal, and local governments to establish or maintain programs to protect or assist witnesses
Develop and disseminate best practice models to assist states and other relevant entities in addressing witness safety.
Document 4: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) to make a video recording of the entire process of its examination and testing
Bars ATF from editing or erasing any such recording.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to render inadmissible as evidence in the prosecution of a federal felony all prior and subsequent statements by an individual during a custodial interrogation that are not electronically recorded
Requires the Attorney General to make a copy of the electronic recording of a custodial interrogation available to the individual.
Document 6: Repeals a provision of the federal criminal code requiring a provider of electronic communication service or remote computing service to disclose to a governmental entity local and long distance telephone connection records
Protects the privacy of subscribers to electronic communication services.
Document 7: Amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age was a motivating factor
Permits a complaining party to rely on any type or form of admissible evidence.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Infrastructure
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to renewable energy initiatives, funding for clean energy projects, and the establishment of infrastructure to support alternative fuels.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to authorize the President to waive the requirement concerning the applicable volume of renewable fuels in gasoline
if the President finds that complying with such requirement in that year is not technologically feasible or that the fuel concerned is not commercially available
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy to provide grants to local educational agencies covering up to 50% of their costs in implementing clean energy projects
that include measures to conserve energy and equip school buildings for renewable power sources.
Document 3: Establishes the Energy Security Fund, to be funded by proceeds deposited in the general fund of the Treasury from fines, penalties, and other funds obtained through enforcement actions for violations of automobile fuel economy standards
to establish a program of grants to expand the availability of alternative fuels to consumers.

topic.name: Veterans Support and Benefits
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing support, education, and assistance for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Transparency for America's Heroes Act - Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to make available redacted records and documents created by the VA.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that any such records or documents created during the two-year period before the enactment of this Act are also made available.
Document 2: Authorizes monthly annuity payments under the Survivor Benefit Plan to a supplemental or special needs trust for a disabled dependent child.
Provides financial support for children of veterans who are incapable of self-support.
Document 3: Establishes the Wounded Warrior K-9 Corps program to provide assistance dogs to veterans with disabilities.
Defines 'assistance dog' and outlines the program's purpose.
Document 4: Modifies preseparation counseling requirements for members of the Armed Forces.
Includes employment assistance and psychological evaluations as part of the transition process.
Document 5: Increases educational assistance payments for veterans pursuing apprenticeship or job training.
Covers multiple educational assistance programs for veterans.
Document 6: Establishes a scholarship program for individuals studying visual impairment or orientation and mobility.
Requires service agreements with the Department of Veterans Affairs after program completion.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Labor to award grants for supporting disadvantaged youth in education and career preparation.
Establishes partnerships for providing comprehensive services to assist youth.

topic.name: Maritime Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures aimed at regulating maritime activities to protect the environment and promote clean energy solutions.

Document 1: Big Oil Bailout Prevention Act of 2011 - Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability of a party responsible for an offshore facility.
Makes this Act effective on April 15, 2010.
Document 2: Clean Low-Emission Authorization Nationwide (CLEAN) Ports Act of 2009 - Directs the Secretary of Transportation to award competitive grants to seaport governing bodies for the acquisition of fuel efficient and low-emission equipment.
Sets aside up to 10% of federal funding for research and development of fuel efficient port vehicle or vessel technologies.
Document 3: Big Oil Bailout Prevention Act of 2013 - Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability of a party responsible for an offshore facility.
from which oil is discharged into or upon navigable waters or adjoining shorelines.
Document 4: Growing American Shipping Act of 2014 - Amends the Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation Act of 2006 to promote the transportation of imports of liquefied natural gas.
to include the promotion of the transportation of exports of the gas from the United States.
Document 5: Protecting Lakes Against Quaggas Act of 2013 or the PLAQ Act of 2013 - Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit the importation or shipment of the species of mussels of the genus Dreissena.
thus covering quagga mussels.
Document 6: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to waive the requirement that Canadian vessels report immediately to the nearest customs facility upon arrival at any U.S. port.
if, in fact, they do not anchor or dock at any harbor within the U.S. customs territory.
Document 7: Ashland Breakwater Light Transfer Act - Adjusts the boundary of the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore in Wisconsin to include the Ashland Harbor Breakwater Light Addition.
Authorizes the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating to operate, maintain, keep, locate, inspect, repair, and replace any federal aid to navigation.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Reporting
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and reporting requirements within various federal agencies and programs.

Document 1: Amends the Jobs Accountability Act to revise deadlines for CBO and GAO comments on federal recovery fund reports.
Terminates reporting requirements with respect to TARP.
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act to repeal reporting requirements for DNA analysis grants.
Repeals annual report requirement on the Police Corps program.
Document 5: Requires federal agencies to prepare and submit consolidated financial statements based on fair-value accrual accounting.
Mandates audits of consolidated financial statements by the Comptroller General.
Document 6: Directs the Comptroller General to include common grounds for sustaining contract protest bids in annual reports to Congress.
Document 7: Directs the President to establish guidelines for measurable goals and performance metrics for U.S. foreign assistance programs.
Requires related reports to Congress from the Comptroller General.

topic.name: Legislation for Regional Development and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at promoting regional development, resource management, and conservation efforts across different areas in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Delta Development Act to include Vernon and Sabine Parishes of Louisiana within the Lower Mississippi Delta region of the Delta Regional Authority.
Document 2: Maximize Offshore Resource Exploration Act of 2008 or the MORE Act of 2008 - Declares without force or effect all federal prohibitions against the expenditure of appropriated funds to conduct natural gas leasing and preleasing activities for any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS).
Document 3: Amends the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 to establish in the Department of Agriculture the Office of Rural Broadband Initiatives.
Document 5: This bill amends the Highlands Conservation Act to reauthorize through FY2021: (1) land conservation partnership projects in the highlands regions of Connecticut, New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania.
Document 7: Amends the Highlands Conservation Act to extend, through FY2024, funding for: (1) land conservation partnership projects in the highlands regions of Connecticut, New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania.
